title: You won't find what you're searching for if you don't know what it is
html:
TL;DR

  • January 2, 2020 micro-note opens with the koan-line "You won't find what you a searching for if you don't know what it is?..." — Yahir at 17, still in Grants Pass, framing the new year as a search problem before listing chores.
  • Bundles the asking the right question disposition with banal FBLA / Chinese-recording / Kim-eyebrow-trip / wrestling-prep todos — early example of self talk wedged into a to do list.
  • One of the earliest written instances of the "what am I actually looking for" meta-question that recurs through the 2021 12 27 whats now year-end reflection and the 2022 seven motivations WHY list.
  • Captures the late-Grants Pass teen era register: high-school logistics + Mandarin self-study + a small philosophical seed at the top of the page.
  • The FBLA / Chinese-recording / wrestling triple-stack is the same pre-OSU resume-stacking that scaffolds the Ford Family Foundation application a year later.

Source quote

"You won't find what you a searching for if you don't know what it is?..."

"-reddit fbla / -finish Chinese recording / -go to Kim's for eyebrows / -order shoe / -get school shit nice / -have school clothes ready / -wrestling stuff ready"

Open questions

  • What was Yahir actually searching for in Jan 2020? The note doesn't answer; the "What's Now?" year end reframes the same question two years later.
  • Was the Chinese recording tied to an FBLA event, the high school Mandarin class, or already pointed at the China chapter arc?

Wikilinks
